title: gh#67: R3.2 — Switch api/profile.service to TokenVault for seal direction
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/67.
Backlog section: Candidate 3 — TokenVault.

## Context

Second PR in Candidate 3. Migrates the API's connect-flow encryption calls to the vault. Smallest behavioral change in the series — just swaps the call shape.

## Scope

- Modify `packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts`:
  - Inject `vault: TokenVault` via service-factory pattern (or pass as param to the connect functions, editorial call)
  - Replace the four inline `encrypt(credentials.X, encryptionKey, 1)` calls (line ~172–175) with `vault.sealTwitterCredentials({ ... })`
  - Replace the OAuth 2.0 `encrypt` calls in the LinkedIn callback path (line ~427+) with `vault.sealOAuth2AccessToken(...)` and `vault.sealOAuth2RefreshToken(...)`
  - Replace the OAuth 2.0 `encrypt` calls in the Facebook callback path (line ~547+) similarly
  - Remove `process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY` reads inside these functions — the vault is constructed once at process start and injected
- Update `packages/api/src/index.ts` (or wherever app composition happens) to construct the vault and inject it
- Update existing tests in `packages/api/src/__tests__/services/profile.test.ts` to inject a fake vault

## Out of scope

- token-refresh-worker (R3.3)
- twitter-delete service (R3.4)
- Publishers (R3.5)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/api build` and test pass
- `rg "encrypt\(" packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts` returns no matches
- `rg "process\.env\.ENCRYPTION_KEY" packages/api/src/services/profile.service.ts` returns no matches

## References

- Depends on: #66
